description: Flight detail page shows the full-page error boundary and the user cannot continue booking.
expectedOutcome: User can view flight details and proceed (e.g. continue to passenger details) even when something fails on the page.
hint: Handle the promise rejection (e.g. .catch) and either set a fallback for extras or guard the Add-ons section so it never reads undefined.
symptom: Full-page error boundary on flight detail; user cannot proceed.
solution: Catch the extras promise rejection in the effect (e.g. .catch) and either set flight.extras to [] on error so the Add-ons section has a safe value, or set extrasError state and in render only show the Add-ons list when flight.extras is defined (e.g. Array.isArray(flight.extras)), otherwise show 'Add-ons unavailable'.
solutionCode: In the extras useEffect, add .catch(() => setFlight((prev) => (prev ? { ...prev, extras: [] } : prev))); so extras is always an array. Or: add state setExtrasError, .catch(setExtrasError), and in render use: {Array.isArray(flight.extras) ? flight.extras.map((opt) => opt.name).join(', ') || 'None' : 'Add-ons unavailable'}
playwrightTestSnippets: test('when flight extras API rejects, error boundary shows fallback', async ({ page }) => { ... });
